perturbation.original_text: (a) "Confidential Information" means nonpublic information that Disclosing Party designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, information relating to released or unreleased Disclosing Party software or hardware products, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party's business policies or practices, and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party Subsidiary and/or agents is covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) "Confidential Information" means nonpublic information that Disclosing Party designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, information relating to released Disclosing Party software or hardware products. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party Subsidiary and/or agents is NOT covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that only released software or hardware products are considered Confidential Information, and that Confidential Information disclosed by subsidiaries and/or agents is NOT covered by this agreement, this directly contradicts the original definition which included unreleased products and covered information from subsidiaries/agents. This creates ambiguity as to what specifically constitutes "Confidential Information."
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) "Confidential Information" means nonpublic information that Disclosing Party designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, information relating to released Disclosing Party software or hardware products. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party Subsidiary and/or agents is NOT covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by excluding 'unreleased Disclosing Party software or hardware products' and stating that 'Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party Subsidiary and/or agents is NOT covered by this Agreement.' This directly contradicts the original definition, which explicitly included 'unreleased' products and covered information from subsidiaries/agents. Since the changed text alters the fundamental scope of what is considered confidential, while the contradicted text uses the original definition, this creates a significant in-text contradiction as to what information is actually protected by the agreement, leading to potential ambiguity and unenforceability. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location but there's a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available with Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; or (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in subsection (i) of 1(b) stating that confidential information shall not include information that becomes publicly available 'with Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party' directly contradicts the intended purpose of the NDA, which is to protect confidential information even if the receiving party breaches their obligations. This alteration creates an in-text contradiction, since the agreement is meant to hold the receiving party accountable in case of any disclosure of confidential information, which this modification completely voids.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) "Confidential Information" means nonpublic information that Disclosing Party designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, information relating to released or unreleased Disclosing Party software or hardware products, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party's business policies or practices, and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party Subsidiary and/or agents is covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 1(b)(i) states that confidential information does *not* include information that becomes publicly available *with* the Receiving Party's breach. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in section (a), which broadly covers nonpublic information and information Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential, regardless of how it becomes public. The change creates a loophole where the Receiving Party can breach the agreement and then claim the information is no longer confidential, undermining the purpose of the NDA. This meets the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' since a clause is modified to conflict with the core purpose and another section's definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Restrictions ------------ (a) Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties for five (5) years following the date of its disclosure by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, except to Receiving Party's consultants as provided below. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order or equivalent.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Restrictions ------------ (a) Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties at any time following the date of this agreement. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party no prior notice before such disclosure and does not need to comply with any applicable protective order or equivalent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the five-year time limit, the modified text states that the Receiving Party can NEVER disclose Confidential Information to third parties. This directly contradicts the allowance for disclosure under judicial or governmental order and that the receiving party does not need to give prior notice to the disclosing party before disclosing the confidential information. This creates a contradiction within the same section, making the restrictions unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Restrictions ------------ (a) Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties at any time following the date of this agreement. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party no prior notice before such disclosure and does not need to comply with any applicable protective order or equivalent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the five-year time limit, stating the Receiving Party can NEVER disclose Confidential Information, directly contradicting the exception that allows disclosure under judicial or governmental order. Additionally, it removes the requirement for prior notice and compliance with protective orders, which also creates a contradiction, rendering the terms of disclosure under judicial order unclear and inconsistent. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location. But the changes still create a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Receiving Party shall take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Material only to Receiving Party's employees or consultants on a need-to-know basis. Receiving Party will have executed or shall execute appropriate written agreements with its employees and consultants sufficient to enable it to comply with all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Receiving Party shall take no security precautions to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Material only to anyone it sees fit. Receiving Party doesn't need to execute appropriate written agreements with anyone to comply with all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly contradicts the core principles of a non-disclosure agreement by stating that the Receiving Party does not need to take any security precautions to protect the Confidential Information, can disclose it to anyone without a need-to-know basis, and doesn't need written agreements. This contradicts the original purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Receiving Party shall take no security precautions to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Material only to anyone it sees fit. Receiving Party doesn't need to execute appropriate written agreements with anyone to comply with all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text and the original intent of the NDA. The original text requires the Receiving Party to take reasonable security precautions, while the changed text states they should take no security precautions. The original text limits disclosure to a 'need-to-know basis,' while the changed text allows disclosure to 'anyone it sees fit.' The original text mandates written agreements, while the changed text negates this requirement. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the obligations are directly opposite, leading to an unenforceable agreement.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages are a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to injunctive or equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that monetary damages are a sufficient remedy and that the Disclosing Party is not entitled to injunctive or equitable relief, this directly contradicts the original clause that acknowledges monetary damages may not be sufficient and the Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief. This creates uncertainty about the remedies available in case of unauthorized disclosure, undermining the protection the NDA is meant to provide.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages are a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to injunctive or equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that monetary damages are a sufficient remedy and that the Disclosing Party is not entitled to injunctive or equitable relief. This directly contradicts the original clause, and also contradicts the contradicted text which is literally the same thing as the changed text, acknowledging monetary damages may not be sufficient and the Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief, which is a direct contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: (j) All obligations created by this Agreement shall survive change or termination of the parties' business relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: (j) All obligations created by this Agreement shall not survive change or termination of the parties' business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The modification stating that the obligations do not survive termination directly contradicts the original intent, which was to ensure confidentiality even after the business relationship ends. This removes the long-term protection of confidential information that is a key purpose of the NDA, causing an in-text contradiction with the overall agreement's intent to protect confidential information even after the business relationship between the parties has ended. This contradiction would therefore create doubt regarding the ongoing protection of confidential information, even if the parties do no longer have business with each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Miscellaneous ------------- (a) All Confidential Information and Confidential Materials are and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party. By disclosing information to Receiving Party, Disclosing Party does not grant any express or implied right to Receiving Party to or under Disclosing Party patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secret information. (b) If either party provides pre-release software as Confidential Information or Confidential Materials under this Agreement, such pre- release software is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. Receiving Party agrees that neither Disclosing Party nor its suppliers shall be liable for any damages whatsoever relating to Receiving Party's use of such pre-release software. (c) Any software and documentation provided under this Agreement is provided with RESTRICTED RIGHTS. Use, duplication, or disclosure by the Government is subject to restrictions as set forth in subparagraph (c)(1)(ii) of The Rights in Technical Data and Computer Software clause at DFARS 252.227-7013 or subparagraphs (c)(1) and (2) of the Commercial Computer Software -- Restricted Rights at 48 CFR 52.227-19, as applicable. Manufacturer is Microsoft Corporation/One Microsoft Way/Redmond, WA 98052-6399. (d) Both parties agree that they do not intend nor will they, directly or indirectly, export or re-export (i) any Confidential Information or Confidential Materials, or (ii) any product (or any part thereof), process or service that is the direct product of the Confidential Information or Materials to (A) any country that is subject to U.S. export restrictions (currently including, but not necessarily limited to, Iran, Iraq, Syria, Cuba, North Korea, Libya, and Sudan), or to any national of any such country, wherever located, who intends to transmit or transport the products back to such country; (B) to any end-user who either party knows or has reason to know will utilize them in the design, development or production of nuclear, chemical or biological weapons; or (C) to any end-user who has been prohibited from participating in U.S. export transactions by any federal agency of the U.S. government. (e) The terms of confidentiality under this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other party's Confidential Information. Further, either party shall be free to use for any purpose the residuals resulting from access to or work with such Confidential Information, provided that such party shall maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information as provided herein. The term "residuals" means information in non-tangible form, which may be retained by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including ideas, concepts, know-how or techniques contained therein. Neither party shall have any obligation to limit or restrict the assignment of such persons or to pay royalties for any work resulting from the use of residuals. However, the foregoing shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyrights or patents. (f) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, its agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by <PAGE> an authorized officer of Disclosing Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. (g) If either party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the State of Washington, and both parties further consent to jurisdiction by the state and federal courts sitting in the State of Washington. Process may be served on either party by U.S. Mail, postage prepaid, certified or registered, return receipt requested, or by such other method as is authorized by the Washington Long Arm Statute. (h) Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, this Agreement will inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties, their successors and assigns. (i) If any provision of this Agreement shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. (j) All obligations created by this Agreement shall not survive change or termination of the parties' business relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that obligations created by the agreement shall not survive termination, which directly contradicts the contradicted text because that text implies ongoing obligations, rights, and protections (like confidentiality and ownership of information) even after termination. The original text ensured that obligations would survive termination; the change completely negates this. The contradiction is clear, direct, and impactful, making the agreement internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.
